info.title: Binder Public API
info.description:
Read-only access to Binder's own value-added layer: public binders (holdings and valuation), a binder's price history, public trade lists, and Binder's normalized card catalog.

The catalog covers every card tracked in Binder, in our normalized cross-game model, with a per-card price history built from our own daily snapshots. It is enough to build a collection app of your own on top of. This API deliberately does not expose a live upstream card or price search passthrough. Card data and pricing come from Scryfall (MTG), PokéWallet (Pokémon), and OPTCG API (One Piece); every response includes an `attribution` field crediting them.

No authentication is required. Only resources their owner has explicitly made public are reachable: a public binder, or a trade list (trade lists are always public by design). Everything else — private or nonexistent — returns 404.

**Errors** use one envelope: `{ "error": { "code": "...", "message": "..." } }`, where `code` is a stable machine-readable string.

**Caching**: every GET returns a weak `ETag`; send it back as `If-None-Match` to get a `304 Not Modified` when the payload is unchanged. Responses also carry `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300, stale-while-revalidate=3600`.

**Pagination**: `/api/v1/cards` is cursor-paginated — follow `next_cursor` until `has_more` is false.

**Rate limiting**: every `/api/v1` request is limited per IP (60 requests/minute). Over the limit returns `429` with a `Retry-After` header and error code `rate_limited`.

**Versioning**: see the Changelog in the documentation for what changed in each release.

tags.name: Binders
tags.description: Public binders and their price history

tags.name: Cards
tags.description: Binder's normalized card catalog and per-card price history

tags.description: A capped, try-it-here demo of the catalog search

get.description: Returns a public binder's metadata, its holdings, and current total USD value. Returns 404 if the binder does not exist or is not public.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Binder UUID, taken from the binder's share link (`/b/<id>`).

example.attribution: Card data and pricing courtesy of Scryfall (MTG), PokéWallet (Pokémon), and OPTCG API (One Piece). Binder is an independent, non-commercial tool and exposes only its own value-added layer (binders, holdings, valuation, price history).

404.description: Binder not found or not public.

error.message: Binder not found or not public

get.summary: Get a public binder's value history
get.description: Returns Binder's own daily price history for a public binder, built from our snapshots (not upstream history). Returns 404 if the binder does not exist or is not public.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Binder UUID, taken from the binder's share link (`/b/<id>`).

example.attribution: Card data and pricing courtesy of Scryfall (MTG), PokéWallet (Pokémon), and OPTCG API (One Piece). Binder is an independent, non-commercial tool and exposes only its own value-added layer (binders, holdings, valuation, price history).

404.description: Binder not found or not public.

error.message: Binder not found or not public

get.description: Returns the cards a user has marked for trade, addressed by the trade list's share token. Trade lists are always public, so any valid token resolves. Returns 404 if the token is unknown.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Trade list share token (UUID), taken from the share link (`/t/<token>`).

example.attribution: Card data and pricing courtesy of Scryfall (MTG), PokéWallet (Pokémon), and OPTCG API (One Piece). Binder is an independent, non-commercial tool and exposes only its own value-added layer (binders, holdings, valuation, price history).

get.description: Searches Binder's normalized card catalog — every distinct card tracked in Binder, across all three games. Served from Binder's own data, not a live upstream search, so a card appears here once someone tracks it. Cursor-paginated: follow `next_cursor` while `has_more` is true.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match on the card name.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of cards to return in this page (1–100).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor. Pass the `next_cursor` from the previous page to fetch the next one; omit for the first page. Do not parse or construct it.

200.description: One page of matching catalog cards, ordered by name. Follow `next_cursor` while `has_more` is true.

example.attribution: Card data and pricing courtesy of Scryfall (MTG), PokéWallet (Pokémon), and OPTCG API (One Piece). Binder is an independent, non-commercial tool and exposes only its own value-added layer (binders, holdings, valuation, price history).

error.message: Unknown game; expected mtg, pokemon, or onepiece

get.summary: Get a catalog card with price history
get.description: Returns one card from the catalog in Binder's normalized model, with Binder's own daily USD price history for it (built from our snapshots, not upstream history), one point per day, all-time. Returns 404 if no Binder user tracks the card.

parameters.name: game
parameters.description: The game the card belongs to.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The card's upstream id (Scryfall UUID, PokéWallet card id, or OPTCG card id).

200.description: The card and its price history.

example.attribution: Card data and pricing courtesy of Scryfall (MTG), PokéWallet (Pokémon), and OPTCG API (One Piece). Binder is an independent, non-commercial tool and exposes only its own value-added layer (binders, holdings, valuation, price history).

get.summary: Get a catalog card's price history
get.description: Returns Binder's own daily USD price history for one catalog card, built from our snapshots (not upstream history), one point per day. Use `days` to bound the window. Returns 404 if no Binder user tracks the card.

parameters.name: game
parameters.description: The game the card belongs to.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The card's upstream id (Scryfall UUID, PokéWallet card id, or OPTCG card id).

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Size of the history window in days (1–365).

200.description: Daily price history for the card.

example.attribution: Card data and pricing courtesy of Scryfall (MTG), PokéWallet (Pokémon), and OPTCG API (One Piece). Binder is an independent, non-commercial tool and exposes only its own value-added layer (binders, holdings, valuation, price history).

get.summary: Try it: search the catalog (capped demo)
get.description: A capped, no-setup way to try the catalog search from these docs. Returns the same normalized cards as `GET /api/v1/cards`, but is limited to **3 pulls per IP per day** so a public docs page can't become an uncapped API. Fill in `q` (and optionally `game`), watch the code sample update with your parameters, then Send to pull real card info. For real usage, call `/api/v1/cards` directly — it is cursor-paginated, allows 60 requests/minute, and has no daily cap. This endpoint lives outside `/api/v1` and is not part of the stable public contract.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match on the card name (e.g. "charizard"). Omit to return the whole catalog.

200.description: Matching catalog cards, plus how many demo pulls remain today.

example.attribution: Card data and pricing courtesy of Scryfall (MTG), PokéWallet (Pokémon), and OPTCG API (One Piece). Binder is an independent, non-commercial tool and exposes only its own value-added layer (binders, holdings, valuation, price history).

error.message: Unknown game; expected mtg, pokemon, or onepiece

429.description: Demo limit reached (3 pulls per IP per day).

error.message: Demo limit reached — 3 card pulls per day from one address. Run Binder locally and call /api/v1/cards directly for unlimited access.

get.description: Returns this OpenAPI 3.0 document. Like every `/api/v1` endpoint it carries a weak `ETag` and is rate-limited per IP.

200.description: The OpenAPI 3.0 specification for this API.

RateLimited.description: Rate limit exceeded (60 requests/minute per IP). Wait for the window to reset, then retry.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded. Max 60 requests per minute.

Game.description: The trading card game a binder or card belongs to.

Price.description: Current prices. Any field may be null when no price is known upstream — null means "unknown", never zero.

Card.description: A card with holding context, as it appears inside a binder or trade list.

image_url.description: Absolute URL to the card image (or null). Safe to fetch from any origin.

finish.description: Which finish the owner holds. A foil holding values against price.usd_foil, a nonfoil against price.usd.

for_trade.description: Present on binder responses only; whether this holding is marked for trade. Omitted from trade-list responses, where every card is for trade.

total_usd.description: Current total USD value of the binder (foils valued at price.usd_foil).

attribution.example: Card data and pricing courtesy of Scryfall (MTG), PokéWallet (Pokémon), and OPTCG API (One Piece). Binder is an independent, non-commercial tool and exposes only its own value-added layer (binders, holdings, valuation, price history).

CatalogCard.description: A card in Binder's normalized cross-game model, without holding context (no quantity, finish, or for_trade — the catalog never exposes who holds a card or how many).

image_url.description: Absolute URL to the card image (or null). Safe to fetch from any origin.

count.description: Number of cards in THIS page (not the catalog total).

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page, or null when has_more is false. Pass it back as `?cursor=`.

attribution.example: Card data and pricing courtesy of Scryfall (MTG), PokéWallet (Pokémon), and OPTCG API (One Piece). Binder is an independent, non-commercial tool and exposes only its own value-added layer (binders, holdings, valuation, price history).

demo.description: Demo cap status for the calling IP.

remaining.description: Pulls left today for this IP.

attribution.example: Card data and pricing courtesy of Scryfall (MTG), PokéWallet (Pokémon), and OPTCG API (One Piece). Binder is an independent, non-commercial tool and exposes only its own value-added layer (binders, holdings, valuation, price history).

PricePoint.description: One day of a card's USD price history.

history.description: All-time daily USD price history, one point per day, oldest first.

attribution.example: Card data and pricing courtesy of Scryfall (MTG), PokéWallet (Pokémon), and OPTCG API (One Piece). Binder is an independent, non-commercial tool and exposes only its own value-added layer (binders, holdings, valuation, price history).

days.description: The requested window size in days.

history.description: Daily USD price history within the window, one point per day, oldest first.

attribution.example: Card data and pricing courtesy of Scryfall (MTG), PokéWallet (Pokémon), and OPTCG API (One Piece). Binder is an independent, non-commercial tool and exposes only its own value-added layer (binders, holdings, valuation, price history).

HistoryPoint.description: One day of a binder's total USD value.

attribution.example: Card data and pricing courtesy of Scryfall (MTG), PokéWallet (Pokémon), and OPTCG API (One Piece). Binder is an independent, non-commercial tool and exposes only its own value-added layer (binders, holdings, valuation, price history).
